content.full_text:
Construction Industry
Advancement Fund

2215 21st Street
Sacramento, CA 95818
916/452-5471

April 15, 1992

Ms. Linda Cassady
Political Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, California  95814

Dear Ms. Cassady:

This letter will follow-up on our telephone conversation earlier
today regarding CIAF's relationship to its grantees and whether or
not CIAF should file Form 690.

As a non-profit organization, CIAF provides grants to various
organizations which promote the construction industry and other
non-profit entities which are not affiliated with any construction
industry association.  These grants are made for the general
support of these organizations.  A portion of these grant funds may be
used to defray staff costs  associated with our grantees'
governmental relations programs.

Several of CIAF's grantees may undertake lobbying efforts as
defined by the Fair Political Practices Commission as part of their
governmental relations programs.  These grantees are listed on Form
645 filed quarterly by CIAF.  At no time are grantee staff or their
activities controlled by CIAF, however.  CIAF does not earmark
grant funds to pay for lobbying activities by its grantees,
designate funds either in support of or opposition to specific
legislative bills, or earmark specific grant funds to influence
administrative actions.

To the extent that CIAF's grantees may undertake activities defined
as  lobbying,  we  suggest  that  your  office  review  these
organizations' reports to ensure that they accurately reflect any
lobbying activities by these organizations.

-2-

I hope that this letter clarifies CIAF's role in relation to its
grantees. Because CIAF neither controls nor earmarks funds for its
grantees' legislative advocacy programs, we do not believe that
state law or FPPC regulations require us to complete form 690. We
would like a written response from your office so indicating so
that we may forward this opinion to the Secretary of State's
Office.

Should you have any questions regarding specific grants to
organizations reported by CIAF on Form 645, please contact us.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Jeff Goldman
Deputy Fund Administrator

CCAP
California
Construction
Advancement
Program

2215 21st Street          Sacramento, California 95818          (916)736-9389

April 6, 1992


Mr. Wayne Imberi
Political Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, California  95814

Dear Mr. Imberi:

We have received several correspondences over the past month from
the Secretary of State's Political Reform division requesting that
we file Form 690, "Amendment to Lobbying Disclosure Act" in
connection with the Form 645 reports that CCAP has filed during
1991.

Barbara Smith of the Secretary of State's Office directed us to
obtain a written opinion from your office that we do not need to
file this form based on our relationship with our grantees.

As a non-profit organization, CCAP provides grants to various
organizations which promote the construction industry.  These
grants are made for the general support of these organizations.  A
portion of these funds may be used to defray staff costs associated
with our grantees' governmental relations programs.

Several of CCAP's grantees may undertake lobbying efforts as
defined by the Fair Political Practices Commission as part of their
governmental relations programs.  These grantees are listed on Form
645, filed quarterly by CCAP.  At no time are grantee staff or
their activities controlled by CCAP, however.  CCAP does not
earmark grant funds to pay for lobbying activities by its grantees,
designate funds either in support of or opposition to specific
legislative bills, or earmark specific grant funds to influence
administrative actions.

To the extent that CCAP's grantees may undertake activities defined
as lobbying, we suggest that your office review these
organizations' reports to ensure that they accurately reflect any
lobbying activities.

-2-

I hope that this letter clarifies CCAP's role in relation to its
grantees. Because CCAP neither controls nor earmarks funds for its
grantees' legislative advocacy programs, we do not believe that
state law or FPPC regulations require us to complete form 690. We
would like a written response from your office so indicating.

Should you have any questions regarding specific grants to
organizations reported by CCAP on Form 645, please contact us.

Sincerely,

Ann Muller
Deputy Fund Administrator

FPPC

APR 1 1992

Construction Industry
Advancement Fund

2215 21st Street
Sacramento, CA 95818
916/452-5471
